description: Lecture discusses demand reduction in multi-unit auctions, focusing on auction formats and equilibrium conditions. It revisits previous topics and introduces new bounds on the price of anarchy, emphasizing the importance of downward sloping valuations in maximizing welfare.
topic_summary: The lecture covers auction formats, equilibrium conditions, and the implications of downward sloping valuations on welfare maximization [t=80.0]. It also discusses the VCG mechanism and clinching auction as methods for implementing these auction formats [t=290.31].
